content.problemStatement:
There are NNN different food items, each providing an essential vitamin AiA_iAi​ and costing BiB_iBi​. There is an integer parameter CCC which represents the value of a single vitamin to you.

You will buy some of the food items to maximize C⋅X−YC \cdot X - YC⋅X−Y (CCC multiplied by XXX minus YYY), where XXX represents the number of **distinct** vitamins eaten, and YYY represents the total cost of all the food items. Find this maximum possible value. It is also possible to buy no food item at all, in which case the answer is simply 000.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN and CCC - the number of food items and the value of a vitamin.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The third line contains NNN integers - B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the maximum possible value of C⋅X−YC \cdot X - YC⋅X−Y as explained in the statement.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : It is optimal to buy all the food items. You will get 333 distinct vitamins and it will cost you a total of 333. Thus, your value is 5⋅3−3=125 \cdot 3 - 3 = 125⋅3−3=12.
Test Case 2 : It is optimal to not buy any food item.
